journals.title: How to Photograph Your Project for Award Submissions

journals.useful_for: client education - helps potential clients understand the value of professional photography for awards; sales pitch material - demonstrates expertise in awards photography; content marketing - positions photographer as industry expert

journals.title: Architectural Photography in Whistler | What's Different
journals.summary: This article explores the unique challenges and considerations of photographing architecture in Whistler's mountain environment. It addresses how altitude light, snow, alpine materials, and the relationship between building and landscape distinguish mountain architectural photography from urban or lowland projects.
key_points: Altitude and light quality affect architectural photography in mountain settings
Snow and alpine materials require special photographic consideration
The relationship between building and landscape is critical in mountain architecture
Whistler's unique environmental conditions create distinct photography requirements
journals.primary_topic: Location-specific architectural photography considerations for mountain/alpine projects
journals.useful_for: client education - explaining specialty services and unique value proposition for mountain/alpine architectural photography

journals.title: Why Great Photography Starts Before the Build Finishes
